text: The da Vinci operating system is used to perform a mesenteric dissection to collect lymph nodes associated with an incidentally found ileal mass. The ileum is divided using a ligature, and the sealed ends are gently opened and aligned for a side-to-side anastomosis.

The surgeon introduces a 60mm intestinal stapler into the open ends of the ileum to perform a side-to-side anastomosis. After firing the stapler, the anastomotic line is visually inspected for hemostasis. The open end of the anastomosis is closed with two additional firings of the stapler. To prevent bowel herniation, the surgeon closes the small mesenteric defect by grasping and securing the edges with an endo loop.
Following resection of the ileum, the patient went on to have an uneventful robotic sigmoid resection.
